SME Mark Scheme · Paper 2 Advanced


1. Use the question-specific mark scheme together with the mark bands.
2. Whenever relevant, carry-over (OFR) marks must be awarded.
3. A candidate may be penalised once for rounding/unit errors across (b) subparts.

Question 1 · Nigeria
1. (a) (i) Define poverty. [2]

  Level                                                                                      Marks

  0        The work does not meet a standard described by the descriptors below.             0

  1        Vague definition.                                                                 1
           The idea that poverty is a condition of low income.

  2        Accurate definition.                                                              2
           An understanding that poverty is the inability of an individual or household to
           meet basic needs; absolute poverty is defined by income below the minimum
           necessary to satisfy basic physical needs, while relative poverty is defined
           relative to the typical standard of living in a society.

1. (a) (ii) Define Human Development Index (HDI). [2]
  Level                                                                                      Marks

  0        The work does not meet a standard described by the descriptors below.             0

  1        Vague definition.                                                                 1
           The idea that HDI measures development in a country.

  2        Accurate definition.                                                              2
           An understanding that the HDI is a composite index created by the UNDP to
           measure economic development, combining indicators of health (life
           expectancy), education (expected and mean years of schooling) and living
           standards (GNI per capita, PPP).


                                                   1                                    SME Predicted Paper
                                                                           SME Mark Scheme P2 · Advanced


1. (b) (i) Percentage change in Nigeria’s HDI between 2010 and 2022. [3]
HDI 2010 = 0.487; HDI 2022 = 0.535.
% change = [(0.535 − 0.487) / 0.487] × 100 [1]
= (0.048 / 0.487) × 100 [1]
Answer = 9.86% [1] (correct units and 2 d.p. required for full marks).

1. (b) (ii) Nominal GDP per capita of Nigeria in 2022. [2]
Nominal GDP = $477 bn; population ≈ 218 million.
GDP per capita = 477,000,000,000 / 218,000,000 [1]
Answer = US$2,188.07 per person [1]

1. (c) Demand and supply · removal of fuel subsidy. [4]

 Level                                                                                        Marks

 0         The work does not meet a standard described by the descriptors below.              0

 1         There is a correct diagram OR an accurate written response.                        1-2
           For a demand and supply diagram with supply shifting left (cost rises as
           subsidy removed) OR an explanation that removing the subsidy raises costs
           and prices [1], reducing equilibrium quantity [1].

 2         There is a correct diagram AND an accurate written response.                       3-4
           For a correct demand and supply diagram showing supply curve shifting up
           to the left AND an explanation that removal of subsidy raises effective price
           to consumers [1], leading to lower quantity consumed [1].


                                                   2                                       SME Predicted Paper
                                                                          SME Mark Scheme P2 · Advanced


1. (d) Exchange rate · naira unification and exporters. [4]

 Level                                                                                     Marks

 0         The work does not meet a standard described by the descriptors below.           0

 1         There is a correct diagram OR an accurate written response.                     1-2
           For an exchange rate diagram with the naira depreciating OR an explanation
           that devaluation makes exports cheaper to foreigners [1], boosting export
           competitiveness [1].

 2         There is a correct diagram AND an accurate written response.                    3-4
           For a correct exchange rate diagram showing the naira falling in value AND
           an explanation that Nigerian exporters benefit from cheaper
           domestic-currency prices to foreign buyers [1], raising export volumes and
           revenues (Marshall–Lerner permitting) [1].

1. (e) AD/AS · infrastructure deficiencies and LRAS. [4]

 Level                                                                                     Marks

 0         The work does not meet a standard described by the descriptors below.           0

 1         There is a correct diagram OR an accurate written response.                     1-2
           For an AD/AS diagram with LRAS failing to shift right OR an explanation that
           unreliable electricity raises costs [1], reducing productive potential and
           limiting long-run growth [1].

 2         There is a correct diagram AND an accurate written response.                    3-4
           For a correct AD/AS diagram showing the LRAS to the left of its potential
           AND an explanation that infrastructure gaps raise production costs [1],
           limiting LRAS and undermining long-term growth [1].

1. (f) Corruption and poverty cycle. [4]

  Level                                                                                       Marks

  0          The work does not meet a standard described by the descriptors below.            0

  1          There is a correct diagram OR an accurate written response.                      1-2
             For a poverty-cycle diagram showing low investment / low human capital OR
             an explanation that corruption diverts resources [1], reducing investment in
             education and health and perpetuating poverty [1].

  2          There is a correct diagram AND an accurate written response.                     3-4
             For a correct poverty-cycle diagram (vicious circle of low income, low saving,
             low investment) AND an explanation that corruption weakens institutions [1],
             deters FDI and undermines human capital accumulation [1].

1. (g) Evaluate AfCFTA as most effective strategy for economic development in Nigeria. [15]
Answers may include:
      • Definitions of economic development, HDI, free trade area.
      • Theory/diagrams: AD/AS, tariff diagram, poverty cycle, trade creation/diversion.
      • Benefits of AfCFTA: larger market of 1.3 bn consumers (Text A, 5); export diversification away
      from oil; economies of scale for manufacturing; regional supply chains; potential FDI attraction.
      • Limitations: deep structural weaknesses: 40% poverty (Text A, 1); high corruption (Text B, 3);
      infrastructure gaps (Text B, 4); youth unemployment >40% (Text B, 5); low HDI of 0.535 (Text B,
      1); falling GNI per capita (Table 1).
      • Alternatives: continued structural reform, removal of fuel subsidy (already done), broader tax
      base, investment in human capital and infrastructure, anti-corruption measures, exchange-rate
      unification (Text A, 4).
      • Synthesis / conclusion: a reasoned judgement · typically that AfCFTA can complement but
      not substitute for fundamental governance and supply-side reform; effectiveness depends on
      Nigeria’s ability to compete.

  Marks          Level descriptor

  0              The work does not meet a standard described by the descriptors below.

  1–3            The response indicates little understanding of the specific demands of the question.
                 Economic theory is stated but it is not relevant.
                 Economic terms are stated but they are not relevant.
                 The response contains no evidence of synthesis or evaluation.
                 The response contains no use of text/data.

  4–6            The response indicates some understanding of the specific demands of the
                 question.
                 Relevant economic theory is described.
                 Some relevant economic terms are included.
                 The response contains evidence of superficial synthesis or evaluation.
                 The response contains limited use of text/data.

        these demands are only partially addressed.
        Relevant economic theory is partly explained.
        Some relevant economic terms are used appropriately.
        Where appropriate, relevant diagram(s) are included.
        The response contains evidence of appropriate synthesis or evaluation but lacks
        balance.
        The response includes some relevant information from the text/data.

10–12   The specific demands of the question are understood and addressed.
        Relevant economic theory is explained.
        Relevant economic terms are used appropriately.
        Where appropriate, relevant diagram(s) are included and explained.
        The response contains evidence of appropriate synthesis or evaluation that is
        mostly balanced.
        The use of information from the text/data is generally appropriate, relevant, and
        applied correctly.

13–15   The specific demands of the question are understood and addressed.
        Relevant economic theory is fully explained.
        Relevant economic terms are used appropriately throughout the response.
        Where appropriate, relevant diagram(s) are included and fully explained.
        The response contains evidence of effective and balanced synthesis or evaluation.
        The use of information from the text/data is appropriate, relevant, and is used to
        formulate a reasoned argument supported by analysis/evaluation.

Question 2 · Brazil
2. (a) (i) Define inflation targeting. [2]

  Level                                                                                     Marks

  0        The work does not meet a standard described by the descriptors below.            0

  1        Vague definition.                                                                1
           The idea that the central bank tries to keep inflation low.

  2        Accurate definition.                                                             2
           An understanding that inflation targeting is a monetary policy framework in
           which the central bank publicly commits to keeping the inflation rate close to
           a pre-announced numerical target, typically using the policy interest rate as
           its main instrument.

2. (a) (ii) Define common access resource. [2]

  Level                                                                                     Marks

  0        The work does not meet a standard described by the descriptors below.            0

  1        Vague definition.                                                                1
           The idea that a common access resource is something that belongs to no
           one in particular.

  2        Accurate definition.                                                          2
           An understanding that a common access resource is one that is
           non-excludable (no one can be prevented from using it) but rivalrous (one
           person’s use reduces availability for others), such as ocean fisheries or the
           Amazon rainforest · often leading to over-use (the tragedy of the commons).


                                                    6                                   SME Predicted Paper
                                                                             SME Mark Scheme P2 · Advanced


2. (b) (i) Average rate of inflation in Brazil, 2018–2023. [2]
Average = (3.7 + 4.3 + 4.5 + 10.1 + 5.8 + 4.6) / 6 [1]
= 33.0 / 6
Answer = 5.50% [1] (correct units and 2 d.p. required).

2. (b) (ii) Percentage depreciation of the real against the US dollar, 2018–2022. [3]
BRL per US$: 2018 = 3.87; 2022 = 5.16.
% depreciation = [(5.16 − 3.87) / 5.16] × 100 [1]
= (1.29 / 5.16) × 100 [1]
Answer = 25.00% depreciation of the real [1]
Alternative (appreciation of the dollar): (1.29 / 3.87) × 100 = 33.33% appreciation of the US dollar.

2. (c) AD/AS · higher Selic rate to reduce inflation. [4]

  Level                                                                                       Marks

  0          The work does not meet a standard described by the descriptors below.            0

  1          There is a correct diagram OR an accurate written response.                      1-2
             For an AD/AS diagram with AD shifting left OR an explanation that higher
             interest rates reduce consumption and investment [1], lowering AD and the
             price level [1].

  2          There is a correct diagram AND an accurate written response.                     3-4
             For a correct AD/AS diagram showing AD shifting left AND an explanation
             that higher rates raise borrowing costs and saving returns [1], reducing AD
             and containing demand-pull inflation [1].

                                                                           SME Mark Scheme P2 · Advanced


2. (d) Common access / externality · Amazon deforestation. [4]

  Level                                                                                       Marks

  0        The work does not meet a standard described by the descriptors below.              0

  1        There is a correct diagram OR an accurate written response.                        1-2
           For a negative production externality diagram with MSC > MPC and welfare
           loss OR an explanation that producers ignore the external cost of
           deforestation [1], over-producing cattle and soy [1].

  2        There is a correct diagram AND an accurate written response.                       3-4
           For a correct externality diagram (or common access resource diagram
           showing unsustainable use) AND an explanation that producers bear no
           private cost for ecosystem damage [1], leading to excessive deforestation
           and welfare loss [1].

2. (e) Bolsa Família and inequality. [4]

  Level                                                                                       Marks

  0        The work does not meet a standard described by the descriptors below.              0

  1        There is a correct diagram OR an accurate written response.                   1-2
           For a Lorenz curve diagram shifting inward towards the line of equality OR an
           explanation that cash transfers raise income of the poorest [1], reducing
           post-transfer inequality [1].

  2        There is a correct diagram AND an accurate written response.                       3-4
           For a correct Lorenz curve diagram showing a shift toward equality AND an
           explanation that direct transfers lift the income of the lowest quintile [1],
           reducing the Gini coefficient and inequality [1].

                                                                                SME Mark Scheme P2 · Advanced


2. (f) Exchange rate · fall in commodity prices and the real. [4]

 Level                                                                                            Marks

 0           The work does not meet a standard described by the descriptors below.                0

 1           There is a correct diagram OR an accurate written response.                          1-2
             For an exchange rate diagram showing the BRL depreciating OR an
             explanation that lower commodity prices reduce export revenues, reducing
             demand for the real [1], which depreciates [1].

 2           There is a correct diagram AND an accurate written response.                3-4
             For a correct exchange rate diagram with demand for BRL shifting left AND
             an explanation that falling commodity exports reduce foreign demand for BRL
             [1], depreciating the currency [1].

2. (g) Evaluate effectiveness of Brazil’s inflation-targeting regime for macroeconomic stability and
inclusive growth. [15]
Answers may include:
      • Definitions of inflation targeting, monetary policy transmission, inclusive growth, Gini coefficient.
      • Theory/diagrams: AD/AS, Phillips curve, Lorenz curve.
      • Strengths: transition from hyperinflation to moderate inflation; anchored expectations; prompt
      response to 2021 inflation spike (Selic raised to 13.75%, Text A, 5); inflation back to 4.6% by
      2023 (Table 2); signals credibility to investors.
      • Limitations: high volatility (inflation 10.1% in 2021, Table 2); tight policy slows growth and can
      worsen unemployment; blunt tool against supply-side shocks (commodity prices, exchange rate);
      may deepen inequality by raising borrowing costs for poorer households.
      • Inclusive growth: Bolsa Família + minimum wage reduced Gini from 0.58 to 0.52 (Text A, 3) ·
      but this is fiscal policy, not monetary; deforestation and environmental damage (Text A, 4);
      green-growth potential (Text B, 5).
      • Synthesis / conclusion: a reasoned judgement · inflation targeting has delivered greater
      price stability but cannot on its own deliver inclusive or green growth; needs to be combined with
      fiscal redistribution and environmental policy.
